questions.question: Explain the four BGP message types and their purposes.
keyPoints: OPEN - Establishes BGP peering session with parameters like AS number, hold time
UPDATE - Advertises new routes or withdraws existing routes
NOTIFICATION - Reports errors and closes BGP session
KEEPALIVE - Maintains neighbor relationship by confirming peer is alive

questions.question: List and briefly explain the well-known mandatory BGP attributes.
keyPoints: ORIGIN - Indicates how route was learned (IGP/EGP/Incomplete)
AS_PATH - Sequence of AS numbers the route has traversed (loop prevention)
NEXT_HOP - IP address of next hop router to reach destination

questions.question: Describe the BGP path selection process with at least 5 criteria.
keyPoints: Highest Local Preference (default 100)
Shortest AS_PATH length
Lowest Origin type (IGP < EGP < Incomplete)
Lowest MED (from same neighboring AS)
Prefer eBGP over iBGP path
Lowest IGP metric to NEXT_HOP
Lowest BGP Router ID

questions.question: What are the differences between iBGP and eBGP?
keyPoints: iBGP: Between routers in same AS; eBGP: Between routers in different ASes
iBGP neighbors typically use loopback addresses; eBGP neighbors use direct interfaces
iBGP does not modify NEXT_HOP by default; eBGP changes NEXT_HOP to self
iBGP routes not advertised to other iBGP peers (split horizon); eBGP routes advertised to both iBGP and eBGP

questions.question: Explain the BGP neighbor state machine with all six states.
keyPoints: IDLE: Initial state, preparing resources, initiating TCP connection
CONNECT: Waiting for TCP connection to complete
ACTIVE: TCP connection established, waiting for OPEN message
OPEN SENT: OPEN message sent, waiting for peer's OPEN
OPEN CONFIRM: OPEN received, waiting for KEEPALIVE
ESTABLISHED: Peering established, exchanging UPDATE messages

questions.question: What is route summarization in BGP and why is it important?
keyPoints: Route summarization (aggregation) combines multiple specific routes into single prefix
Benefits: Reduces routing table size, minimizes update messages, improves stability
Can cause sub-optimal routing and hide path changes
Use 'aggregate-address' command with AS_SET to include attribute information

questions.question: Explain the purpose and working of BGP route reflection.
keyPoints: Route reflection solves iBGP full mesh requirement in large AS
Route reflector (RR) receives routes from clients and reflects to other peers
Clients peer only with RR, not all iBGP routers
Non-client RR peers must mesh with all other RR
Uses originator ID and cluster-list attributes to prevent loops
